title: 清洁女工之死
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\209-清洁女工之死-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
At the Weatherby residence, Mrs. Weatherby discusses her fatigue and her feelings of being a burden with Dierdre (Dilly). After Dierdre accidentally knocks over a bronze statue while rushing to get eggnog, Mr. Weatherby enters and criticizes Dierdre’s clumsiness and her dog's barking. During their conversation, Mr. Weatherby asks Edith Weatherby about the investigation into Mrs. Upward's death. Edith notes that the police are at a loss regarding the motive and the identity of the killer. Mr. Weatherby suggests a vagrant might be responsible and advises Dierdre to lock the doors, while Edith mentions that Mrs. MacKenzie, a maid, worked for Mrs. Upward. After Mr. Weatherby leaves, Maud (Mrs. Moore) delivers eggnog to Mrs. Weatherby, whom she observes with an imposing presence.

Meanwhile, Hercule Poirot returns to Broadhurst, feeling exhausted by his attempts to discern a pattern in the recent murders. At the Long Grass Hotel, he observes Robin Upward struggling to write a play. Muriel Sampson enters in a panic, reporting that Johnny has not returned from the post office. Morley subsequently searches the lounge for agricultural department forms, creating a mess of various items on the floor. Disturbed by the lack of order, Poirot begins methodically tidying the scattered belongings. His work is interrupted by a phone call from Inspector Spence. Spence reveals new evidence: a witness from the Broadhurst post office saw a blonde woman, not Dierdre Henderson, entering the house on the night of the murder. This shifts the suspicion back to either Eve Carpenter or Sheila Lund, leaving Poirot in stunned silence.
